title: Asian Growth Companies With High Insider Ownership For June 2026
content:
As Asian markets navigate the complexities of geopolitical developments and economic shifts, investor focus has increasingly turned towards growth companies with substantial insider ownership. In such a climate, stocks that demonstrate strong insider commitment often signal confidence in their long-term potential, making them attractive considerations for those seeking resilience and growth amidst market volatility.

Let's dive into some prime choices out of the screener.

Leader Harmonious Drive Systems

Simply Wall St Growth Rating: ★★★★☆☆

Overview: Leader Harmonious Drive Systems Co., Ltd. operates in the precision drive systems industry, with a market cap of CN¥60.04 billion.

Operations: Leader Harmonious Drive Systems Co., Ltd. has revenue segments totaling CN¥60.04 billion in the precision drive systems industry.

Insider Ownership: 38.6%

Earnings Growth Forecast: 23.9% p.a.

Leader Harmonious Drive Systems has demonstrated robust growth, with earnings increasing by 142.9% over the past year and revenue for Q1 2026 rising to ¥140.13 million from ¥98.03 million a year ago. Despite its highly volatile share price, revenue is expected to grow at 24.7% annually, outpacing the Chinese market's growth rate of 16.4%. However, future earnings growth is projected at a slower pace than the market average, and return on equity remains low at an estimated 8%.

Simply Wall St Growth Rating: ★★★★☆☆

Overview: Jiangsu Sidike New Materials Science & Technology Co., Ltd. (SZSE:300806) specializes in the production of advanced polymer materials and has a market cap of CN¥37.60 billion.

Operations: Revenue segments for SZSE:300806 include advanced polymer materials production, contributing significantly to its financial profile.

Story Continues

Insider Ownership: 38.1%

Earnings Growth Forecast: 55.9% p.a.

Jiangsu Sidike New Materials Science & Technology has shown impressive earnings growth of 43.7% over the past year, with forecasts predicting a significant annual increase of 55.9%, surpassing the Chinese market average. Despite this, its return on equity is expected to be modest at 12.8%. Recent financial results reveal Q1 revenue rose to ¥757.19 million from ¥670.78 million year-on-year, while a private placement aims to raise CNY 300 million for strategic initiatives amidst share price volatility.
